items.title: Tesla claims Cybercab driving employees at Giga Texas — in a parking lot
items.topic: Tesla claims Cybercab driving employees at Giga Texas — in a parking lot
items.summary: Tesla claims to be testing its Cybercab, a wheelchair-accessible robotaxi, at its Giga Texas facility. The vehicle is reportedly being driven by employees in a parking lot, indicating early-stage testing. This development highlights Tesla's ongoing efforts to expand its autonomous vehicle capabilities and accessibility features.

items.title: Tesla says it’s building a wheelchair-accessible robotaxi in Texas
items.topic: Tesla says it’s building a wheelchair-accessible robotaxi in Texas
items.summary: Tesla is developing a wheelchair-accessible robotaxi in Texas, aimed at enhancing accessibility in its autonomous vehicle fleet. The company's focus on inclusivity and advanced technology underscores its commitment to expanding the capabilities of its robotaxi service.

items.title: Tesla Says It’s Building a Wheelchair-Accessible Robotaxi
items.topic: Tesla Says It’s Building a Wheelchair-Accessible Robotaxi
items.summary: Tesla is building a wheelchair-accessible robotaxi in Texas, emphasizing its commitment to inclusivity in autonomous transportation. The vehicle is designed to accommodate wheelchair users, aligning with Tesla's broader goals in the robotaxi market.

items.title: Uber’s robotaxi lobbying effort puts it on a collision course with Waymo
items.topic: Uber’s robotaxi lobbying effort puts it on a collision course with Waymo
items.summary: Uber's lobbying efforts for robotaxi regulations are creating tensions with Waymo. Uber is pushing for more favorable regulations, which could impact the competitive landscape of autonomous vehicle deployment and regulatory standards.
items.abstract: Uber's lobbying efforts for robotaxi regulations are creating tensions with Waymo. Uber is pushing for more favorable regulations, which could impact the competitive landscape of autonomous vehicle deployment and regulatory standards.

items.title: New bill would ban Tesla’s camera-only Robotaxi, not Waymo
items.topic: New bill would ban Tesla’s camera-only Robotaxi, not Waymo
items.summary: A new bill proposes to ban Tesla's camera-only robotaxi, while allowing Waymo's sensor-equipped vehicles. This legislation highlights the ongoing regulatory challenges and technological differences between major players in the autonomous vehicle industry.
items.abstract: A new bill proposes to ban Tesla's camera-only robotaxi, while allowing Waymo's sensor-equipped vehicles. This legislation highlights the ongoing regulatory challenges and technological differences between major players in the autonomous vehicle industry.

items.title: LLM-assisted screening method for large-scale transportation model calibration
items.topic: LLM-assisted screening method for large-scale transportation model calibration
items.summary: Researchers have developed an LLM-assisted screening method for large-scale transportation model calibration, published in Nature. This method leverages large language models to improve the accuracy and efficiency of transportation models, which is crucial for optimizing autonomous vehicle operations and urban planning.
items.abstract: Researchers have developed an LLM-assisted screening method for large-scale transportation model calibration, published in Nature. This method leverages large language models to improve the accuracy and efficiency of transportation models, which is crucial for optimizing autonomous vehicle operations and urban planning.

items.title: Apple sues OpenAI, alleging artificial intelligence company stole trade secrets
items.topic: Apple sues OpenAI, alleging artificial intelligence company stole trade secrets
items.summary: Apple has filed a lawsuit against OpenAI, alleging that the artificial intelligence company stole trade secrets. This legal action could have significant implications for the development and regulation of AI technologies, particularly in the context of autonomous vehicles and other AI-driven applications.
items.abstract: Apple has filed a lawsuit against OpenAI, alleging that the artificial intelligence company stole trade secrets. items.title: UniDrive: A Unified Vision-Language and Grounding Framework for Interpretable Risk Understanding in Autonomous Driving
items.topic: UniDrive: A Unified Vision-Language and Grounding Framework for Interpretable Risk Understanding in Autonomous Driving
items.summary: The arXiv paper titled UniDrive proposes a unified visual-language and grounding framework for interpretable risk understanding in autonomous driving, addressing the trade-off between temporal reasoning and spatial precision in existing methods. UniDrive combines a temporal reasoning branch with a high-resolution perception branch to preserve fine-grained spatial details. This framework is relevant for AV research as it enhances the interpretability of risk understanding in autonomous driving. The paper was published on June 23, 2026.
items.abstract: The arXiv paper titled UniDrive proposes a unified visual-language and grounding framework for interpretable risk understanding in autonomous driving, addressing the trade-off between temporal reasoning and spatial precision in existing methods. items.title: Adaptive Cross-Modal Fusion with Sparse Attention for Pedestrian Crossing Intention Prediction
items.topic: Adaptive Cross-Modal Fusion with Sparse Attention for Pedestrian Crossing Intention Prediction
items.summary: The arXiv paper titled Adaptive Cross-Modal Fusion with Sparse Attention for Pedestrian Crossing Intention Prediction proposes a multimodal framework called ADAPT for predicting pedestrian crossing intention. ADAPT jointly models local and global visual context with temporal motion dynamics, processing four spatially aligned visual modalities and ego-vehicle speed, pedestrian bounding boxes, and skeleton pose information. This framework is relevant for AV research as it improves pedestrian crossing intention prediction, a safety-critical task for autonomous driving. The paper was published on July 14, 2026.
items.abstract: The arXiv paper titled Adaptive Cross-Modal Fusion with Sparse Attention for Pedestrian Crossing Intention Prediction proposes a multimodal framework called ADAPT for predicting pedestrian crossing intention. items.title: Handling the Hidden: Occlusion-Aware Motion Prediction for Autonomous Vehicles
items.topic: Handling the Hidden: Occlusion-Aware Motion Prediction for Autonomous Vehicles
items.source: IEEE Open Journal of Intelligent Transportation Systems
items.summary: The IEEE Open Journal of Intelligent Transportation Systems paper titled Handling the Hidden: Occlusion-Aware Motion Prediction for Autonomous Vehicles proposes an extension to the MultIAMP framework to handle occlusions using real perception inputs. The approach systematically detects, processes, and anticipates the behavior of occluded areas, enabling safer navigation. This paper is relevant for AV research as it addresses the challenge of limited visibility due to obstructions in autonomous vehicle motion prediction. The paper was published on March 25, 2026.
